hints: Use an if/elif/else chain that returns a string. Start by checking the highest grade: if score >= 90: return "A".
Order matters in an elif chain — the first true condition wins. If you check >= 70 before >= 90, every high score becomes a C. Go from highest threshold to lowest.
Because elif chains short-circuit, you don't need upper bounds. By the time you check elif score >= 80, you already know score < 90, so >= 80 alone means 80-89.
The final case is else: return "F" — anything that didn't match 60 or above. Use >= at each boundary so 90, 80, 70, 60 land in the higher grade.
